ops.title: Layer 1 completion state + axum pivot + seam contract
ops.body:
# cc-squash Layer 1 — COMPLETE (foundation: daemon + CLI + RelayCore)

Layer 1 ("daemon + CLI + RelayCore transparent passthrough") is **done and CI-green** on `main`. Two processes, one tag: a Go control plane (`ccs`, consuming `github.com/yasyf/fusekit` v0.9.0) supervises a Rust data plane (`ccs-proxy`), a fail-open streaming relay at `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`.

## THE PIVOT (durable — do NOT reintroduce pingora)
The Rust data plane is **axum 0.7 (inbound) + reqwest 0.12 (rustls upstream)**, not pingora. axum+reqwest eliminates the old `PrematureBodyEnd` framing bug **by construction**: the relay buffers the full request body and hands complete `Bytes` to reqwest, which derives `Content-Length` from exactly those bytes. **`reqwest` MUST stay `default-features = false`** — default features enable gzip/brotli/zstd/deflate auto-decompression that strips `Content-Encoding`/`Content-Length` and corrupts the verbatim relay (a CI lockfile guard checks no `flate2`/`brotli`/`zstd` crept in). Response streamed via `axum::body::Body::from_stream(upstream.bytes_stream())`; the fail-open panic boundary is tower-http `CatchPanicLayer`.

## Cardinal invariant (ccs-proxy only — INVERTS the repo "crash on the unexpected" rule)
Fail-open to identity. The relay must never crash on the hot path. Any error/uncertainty forwards the original / passes through. **Unknown / expired / absent `/s/<token>` ⇒ fail-open passthrough, never 404** (a stale `$(ccs url)` must still work). Hot-path modules (`relay`, `forward`, `demux`, `seam`, `synth/*`) carry `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used))]` (enforced by CI clippy).

## The proxy.sock seam (Go↔Rust, line-JSON, `"type"` envelope)
Go BINDS + listens; the Rust child connects (spawned with `--socket`). Rust→Go once: `{"type":"register","port","version","pid"}`. Go→Rust: `mint{token,config}` / `evict{token}` / `shadow{on}` / `kill{on}` / `shutdown`. Per-session config arrives over the seam — **no figment/TOML parser in Rust**. Fail-open both sides; a bare connection drop is non-fatal. The seam task is the SOLE writer to the proxy's `sessions`/`kill`/`shadow` state.

## Cold start + port stability (Phase 3)
`ccs url` → `proc.Spawn.EnsureRunning` brings up the Go daemon → its post-bind goroutine spawns the Rust child → child binds `127.0.0.1:0`, sends `register` → Go caches port + `state.AtomicWrite`s `daemon.port`, builds the `proc.Supervisor` (`Validate()` at wire time), starts the 10s supervise tick → `OpMint` mints a token, pushes it over the seam → `ccs url` prints `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/s/<token>`. **Prior-port re-bind:** a crashed proxy is respawned with `--port <prior>` so live URLs survive (`Reconcile(ChildDied)` keeps `proxyPort`; `Reconcile(Respawned)` re-pushes live tokens). **Version-skew:** the daemon supervises the proxy at `ProxyVersion()` (a Cargo/Go cross-toolchain normalization), NOT `version.String()`, or it flap-replaces every tick; `version_test.go` pins `proxyDevVersion` to the Cargo workspace version.

## Live-proven (real `claude` through the two-process stack)
- **Exp C:** `claude -p` round-trips forward correctly — `POST /v1/messages decision=forward status=200`, correct answers, multi-token streaming, **no 401** (auth headers ride verbatim; `_CLAUDE_CODE_ASSUME_FIRST_PARTY_BASE_URL=1` is the first-party gate, set by `ccs env`/`ccs run` alongside `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` + `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=true`).
- **Synth short-circuit:** a `/compact`-shaped body (the marker literal `CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools.` twice, in the last user turn, `max_tokens<=20000`, no `tool_choice`) gets a LOCAL `<summary>` SSE (`decision=synth`, no upstream). The `<summary>` is still the **Phase-0 placeholder** — the real working-state summary is Layers 2–6.
- Control surface (`ccs kill on|off|status`, `ccs status`, `ccs stop`) proven on the real stack.

## Bugs the REAL two-process stack caught (mocked unit tests missed all 5; fixed + regression-tested)
1. cold-machine `ENOENT` — `client.EnsureRunning` must `EnsureStateDir()` before `proc.Spawn` opens the daemon log.
2. first-start double-spawn race — `bringUp` gates the supervise loop on `proxyReady` (bounded by `proxyStartupGrace`) so the first tick can't fire `Reconcile(ChildDied)` and spawn a second proxy.
3. version-skew replace-loop — see `ProxyVersion()` above.
4. `ccs kill`/`shadow` not reflected in status — `handleKill`/`handleShadow` refresh `status.json` via one `publishStatus()` codepath; the daemon's last-pushed value is the single source of truth.
5. `ccs stop` orphaned the proxy — `serve()` teardown sends an explicit seam `shutdown` frame (`policy.Shutdown()` + `WaitGone(3s)`) after `wg.Wait()` and before the deferred `seam.Close()`. A bare seam drop (daemon CRASH, no frame) still leaves the proxy serving standalone — fail-open preserved (proven distinct in logs: "seam received shutdown" vs "seam closed; serving standalone").

## Release pipeline — wired but BLOCKED on secrets
`.github/workflows/release.yml` (push tags `v*`) is hand-rolled (mirrors cc-pool, not goreleaser): `verify-on-main` gate → `build` (macos-15: universal Go `ccs` via lipo with ldflags injecting `github.com/yasyf/fusekit/version.{Version,Commit}`, + universal Rust `ccs-proxy`; Developer-ID signs + notarizes BOTH; one tarball) → `bump-formula` (renders ONE `CcSquash` formula installing both binaries + a `service do` block, publishes to `yasyf/homebrew-tap`). Validated statically only (actionlint + heredoc render + `brew style`). **The first real `v0.1.0` tag is BLOCKED:** the cc-squash repo has NONE of the 6 required secrets (`HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`, `MACOS_SIGN_P12`, `MACOS_SIGN_PASSWORD`, `MACOS_NOTARY_KEY`, `MACOS_NOTARY_KEY_ID`, `MACOS_NOTARY_ISSUER_ID`); cc-pool has them at repo level, and `yasyf` is a USER account (no org secrets), so they must be copied per-repo first.

## Deferred to the user (cannot auto-run)
1. interactive `/compact` acceptance (the relay emits a valid SSE; "CC accepts it" needs a real interactive `/compact`).
2. first release — add the 6 secrets, then tag `v0.1.0` on `main`.
3. `ccs service install` — meaningful only post-`brew install` (the LaunchAgent must point at the brew binary, not a dev/temp path). `ccs service` builds `service.Agent{Label:"com.yasyf.cc-squash", Formula:"cc-squash", Args:["daemon"], LogPath, Env:{PATH}}` and consumes `Agent.StatusLines()`.

## Build / toolchain gotchas
- `cargo`/`rustc` at `~/.cargo/bin` are NOT on the non-interactive PATH — every Rust shell call needs `export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"`. Go via mise, `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto`.
- fusekit v0.9.0: consume `proc`+`state`+`service`+`version` (`mountd` unused). `proc.SingleEntrant` is a struct with `Listen() (net.Listener, *os.File, error)` — the lock is held for the listener's life and NEVER unlinked. `proc.Policy` = 8 methods. `proc.Spawn{...Override}` (Override injects a fake child in tests). `state.Dir{App}`→`Root()/Path(leaf)/Ensure()` + `state.AtomicWrite`. `service.Agent.Formula` (not `FormulaName`); no `Status()`/`KeepAlive` fields (baked into the plist template).
- Local integration test (build-tagged, excluded from CI): `cargo build -p ccs-proxy && (cd go && go test -tags integration ./internal/integration/ -v)`. Test-only knobs: `CCS_SUPERVISE_INTERVAL` (shrink the supervise tick).
- Reference repos: cc-pool `/Users/yasyf/Code/claude-pool` (the port source), fusekit `/Users/yasyf/Code/fusekit`.

## Next: Layer 2 — the cache-economics engine
`ccs-economics` / `ccs-policy` (segmentation, NPV keep-vs-evict, the reversible squash ladder) per `.claude/research/cc-squash-build-plan.md` §3/§7. The real Interceptor slots in at the `decide()` call site in `relay::serve` (wrap it in a per-call `tokio::time::timeout` → forward-original on timeout; `CatchPanicLayer` already covers panics).
ops.when: before working on Layer 2+ or modifying the Rust relay (crates/ccs-proxy), the Go control plane (go/internal/control,supervisor,proxyseam), the proxy.sock seam, or the release workflow
